item.title: ‘Anthropic’s Safety Superpower’ 摘要
item.description:
Ben Thompson critiques Anthropic's policy of silently altering its Claude models to enforce its own safety preferences, arguing this validates concerns about the company being a supply chain risk. He notes Anthropic believes only it should develop frontier AI, effectively seeking control over AI generally. Thompson compares Anthropic to a religious organization with true believers on a mission, contrasting its fervor with other tech companies' cultural obsessions. He warns that Anthropic's conviction about building superintelligence, combined with its willingness to unilaterally impose its values, poses unique risks beyond typical corporate overreach.
标签: Anthropic, AI safety, AI policy, frontier models, supply chain risk, corporate culture, superintelligence, LLMs, Stratechery, Ben Thompson

item.title: Writing Prolog with ChatGPT 摘要
item.description:
John D. Cook experiments with using ChatGPT to write a SWI Prolog program that solves a chess puzzle: placing a queen, king, rook, bishop, and knight on a 4x4 board so no piece attacks another. ChatGPT generates correct Prolog code that finds all 16 solutions. The author notes that Prolog's stable syntax makes it well-suited for LLM code generation, contrasting with newer languages like Lean where library refactoring complicates AI assistance. The post highlights the potential of using LLMs to write Prolog for logic puzzles, which can translate to real-world constraint satisfaction problems.
标签: Prolog, ChatGPT, LLM, code generation, chess puzzle, logic programming, SWI Prolog, constraint satisfaction, AI-assisted programming

item.title: WorkOS Launches Auth.md — an Open Protocol for Agent Registration 摘要
item.description:
WorkOS launches Auth.md, an open protocol enabling AI agents to programmatically register with services via a machine-readable Markdown file at the service root. This allows agents to discover OAuth metadata, parse required scopes, and authenticate seamlessly. WorkOS AuthKit offers native support, providing a standardized, secure login method for AI tools. The protocol was introduced at the MCP Night: Agent Night keynote.
标签: AI, authentication, OAuth, protocol, WorkOS, Auth.md, AI agents, security, open-source, developer tools

item.title: The European Commission Ruled Months Ago That Google’s Integration of Gemini in Android Violates the DMA 摘要
item.description:
The European Commission has proposed measures under the DMA requiring Google to allow third-party AI services system-level integration on Android, including custom wake words, screen context access, local model hardware access, and free APIs. Critics argue this could lead to security risks and that Google may withdraw Gemini integration in the EU instead of complying. The article contrasts Google's 'ship first, ask forgiveness' approach with Apple's cautious strategy of withholding features to avoid non-compliance.
标签: EU, DMA, Android, Google, Gemini, AI, regulation, antitrust, third-party access, security

item.title: AI's Brokenomics 摘要
item.description:
The US government forced Anthropic to disable its Mythos and Fable AI models due to national security concerns, after Amazon researchers jailbroke Fable's guardrails and China allegedly accessed Mythos. Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei downplayed the risks, leading to export controls banning non-US citizens from using the models. The incident highlights the consequences of Anthropic's hype around 'too dangerous to release' models, which were quickly jailbroken. Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella's comments suggest hyperscalers may be questioning AI labs' dominance, while the government's actions indicate ongoing tensions with Anthropic.
标签: Anthropic, AI regulation, export controls, national security, jailbreak, AI safety, hyperscalers, Microsoft, Amazon, AI hype

item.title: Quaternion Rotations, Claude, and Lean 摘要
item.description:
John D. Cook received a typo report for a year-old blog post on quaternion-rotation matrix conversion. Instead of fixing it manually, he asked Claude Sonnet 4.6 Medium to write Lean 4 code verifying the two theorems. Claude detected a conflict between the Python code and LaTeX-generated SVG alt text, correctly using Python as ground truth. After four iterations of error feedback, Claude produced complete Lean code proving the rotation matrix is orthogonal (RᵀR=I) and verifying the Chiaverini–Siciliano square-root formulas. The post highlights AI-assisted formal verification and the challenge of keeping LaTeX and code consistent.
标签: Lean, Claude, quaternions, rotation matrices, formal verification, AI-assisted programming, theorem proving, Mathlib, LaTeX, Python

item.title: JAX: commitment issues 摘要
item.description:
This article investigates a performance issue in JAX where array lookups on CPU-allocated arrays take over a second per item. The root cause is that arrays created with `jax.default_device` context manager are not committed to that device, allowing JAX to move them to the GPU (the default device) during indexing. Using `jax.device_put` to explicitly commit the array to the CPU resolves the issue, reducing lookup times from ~1.2s to under 0.0002s. The author hypothesizes that JAX may be moving the entire parent array to the GPU on each access, causing the slowdown.
标签: JAX, performance, GPU, CPU, device management, array indexing, debugging, machine learning, Python
